title: Whats next for evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by justyourlocalme » Mon Nov 22, 2021 11:08 pm 3.0 + 1.0 may be the last rebuild, but it cant be the end. Does anyone know whats next for eva?

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Fri Nov 26, 2021 10:42 am A Netflix live-action adaptation. Kidding, but who's to say that Eva is immune to this treatment. We also have possibilities with a spin-off from Rebuild, although a series without Shinji or a re-iteration of the same thing lacks any real gravitas.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Sat Nov 27, 2021 10:52 pm I'm hoping for a video game that's not a dating sim.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by ::KL7:: » Sun Nov 28, 2021 1:07 am backseatjesus wrote: I'm hoping for a video game that's not a dating sim.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Sun Nov 28, 2021 12:22 pm ::KL7:: wrote: I agree, I would actually enjoy an Evangelion video game, does anyone know if there is one in the works? Or why there aren’t many Evangelion fighting/action games for console/PC I seem to recall a couple of older ones and I believe I’ve heard of a VR Evangelion game, but Ive always wondered why there aren’t any (correct me if I’m wrong) recent games? (PS3 and up).

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Sun Nov 28, 2021 6:06 pm ::KL7:: wrote: Or why there aren’t many Evangelion fighting/action games for console/PC I seem to recall a couple of older ones and I believe I’ve heard of a VR Evangelion game, but Ive always wondered why there aren’t any (correct me if I’m wrong) recent games? (PS3 and up).

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Wed Dec 08, 2021 12:19 pm Whatever happens after rebuild, I hope the next film/series/whatever introduces a new cast. NGE gave us Shinji, Rei and Asuka, and showed the trauma they went through. Rebuild gave us a rehash of the same characters going through similar arcs. It's been 26 years of the same three pilots being tortured. I like the three of them, but after the end of Rebuild, I never want to see them again unless it's in a video game. Not sure I can sit through another 10+ hours of the Shinji/Rei/Asuka dynamic again.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Dec 08, 2021 1:08 pm I'm sure it would be tolerable to watch, but I agree, Eva needs to break away from its established cast and show that the themes of the show can very much work with different worlds and people, lest it share the fate of Getter Robo of just living off of references to an increasingly dated original and fading into obscurity as a result.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by EvaChero » Wed Dec 08, 2021 1:44 pm dang, I'd really like a shooter game in the Eva world myself. Still....a semi interesting story could come about with some kind of alien invasion...with an aged Shinji forced to find a way to rebuild the Eva's or an Eva-like technology in order to combat the alien bio-mechas... but I'm afraid that's a well trodden path. Of course watching Shinji squirm a little in the "Gendo" role might be interesting. Just have to wait and see what happens I guess. Too many changes and it might lose it's flavor but I'm willing to give anything a try if it's well thought out..... SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 08, 2021 3:35 pm IronEvangelion wrote: Whatever happens after rebuild, I hope the next film/series/whatever introduces a new cast.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by Blockio » Thu Dec 09, 2021 1:51 am Mr. Tines wrote: That will be the thing that requires some subtlety. Just having psychologically weird alien monstrosity of the week and depressed pilots (hello, Fafner ) aren't as clear a delimiter as Earthnoid/Colonial conflicts and a masked adversary are.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Thu Dec 09, 2021 6:40 am Mr. Tines wrote: there is some scope for alternate takes in the space between NGE and the other super-robots that were clearly influenced by the zeitgeist in one way or another, so long as there is some agreement as to what the common threads are.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by FXArmaros98 » Mon Feb 07, 2022 9:46 am Well there could a lot a possibilities about what could the next Evangelion work: there could release some kind of Rebuild's spin-off like manga and videogame, 3.0+1.0 could get some space in the plot of some possibile future Super Robot Wars's game, also Khara one day could make the animated adaptation of Evangelion ANIMA. I wonder if can be possibile if sooner or later a Live Action could ever be released? I remember that in a interview of 2016 Anno said that he wanted that Evangelion could becoming more like Gundam's franchise, so with multiple timeline/anime's series that doesn't interact with each other because they have different directors. So I guess that one day Anno will let the future of Evangelion in the hands of a new generation of directors that can be assumed by the Khara, if that really happens then we could even see a very different version of Shinji, the Evangelions and the rest of cast or can even be that we will not see Shinji, Asuka, Misato, Rei, Gendo ecc because the new directors will proposed a completely new cast.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 08, 2022 10:23 am Well for now we need several production books, like CRC, storyboards etc. Really looking forward to that.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by FXArmaros98 » Mon Feb 14, 2022 6:01 am Look this https://www.eva-info.jp/16179

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Mon Sep 18, 2023 2:34 pm Seemingly we don't have a thread for general merchandise release discussion, so here goes. https://www.eva-project.jp/pachi-eva_fan_cp2023/ Pachinko artwork  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri Sep 22, 2023 5:48 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: This new pachinko features artwork that is... obviously derived from NGE, not NTE. Asuka (Sohryu?) has properly reddish hair, not Shikinami's strawberry blonde. I'm pretty sure those are the NGE plugsuits and Evas too. And Mari is nowhere to be seen. As I'm sure you all know, merchandising trends have shifted massively over the years and they were always done to reflect fan preference and thus market demand. Now that NTE is over, have fans lost interest in it and returned to the overaching NGE legacy? Moreover, pachinko has long been the No. 1 source of merch income and in a way is a major advertisement for the overall franchise. While this could of course be just merch, it could indicate market trends. After all, during the 2007-2021 period when NTE was "the face" of the overall Evangelion franchise, pretty much all merch reflected its aesthetics. Mari's enlargement in the story was driven by fan demand , then she was pretty popular in merch after 2.0 released, likewise for Kaworu's own outsized (in comparison to NGE) role in 3.0. Then NTE was reflected in merch up for another decade.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by Clowncarcrash » Sat Sep 23, 2023 6:04 am I'm really surprised we haven't seen any new projects yet. To me, the ending to the Rebuilds was kind of gesturing we could see so many new alternate realities and creative takes on the world and the characters.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sun Sep 24, 2023 10:38 pm This is mostly because of franchise exhaustion at this point. Evangelion was EVERYWHERE in Japan, 3.0+1.0 was a huge sucesss, and all of that was 2 and a half year ago. 3.0+1.0 was on the media and they kept releasing statements and making events and all for over half a year. Eva showed up on Newtype and such again more than once, and this year again, and then we had those two OVAs. I think they're simply waiting for the right time to announce something.

[]: Re: Whats next for evangelion?             Quote Post by Clowncarcrash » Mon Sep 25, 2023 3:05 pm Ah, it's hard for me to acknowledge franchise exhaustion when there was a nine year gap between 3.0 and 3.0 + 1.0. But maybe I'm not considering what it was like in Japan.
